paradigms.name: Market Sizing
paradigms.coreIdea: Estimate the total economic opportunity for a product, category, or segment by decomposing population, penetration, and price into multiplicative drivers.
whenItApplies: Question asks 'how big is the market for X'
Investment or entry decision depends on absolute revenue potential
Need to bound a forecast before committing to a plan
Comparing opportunity size across geographies or segments

subVariants.name: Top-Down Sizing
subVariants.keyMechanism: Start from published industry totals and apply successive share filters.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Size the U.S. pet-insurance market by taking total pet spend and multiplying by insured penetration.

subVariants.name: Bottom-Up Sizing
subVariants.keyMechanism: Build from units-per-customer times customers times price, then aggregate.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Size a B2B SaaS opportunity as addressable firms times seats per firm times ACV.

subVariants.name: TAM / SAM / SOM Decomposition
subVariants.keyMechanism: Nest total, serviceable, and obtainable markets to distinguish theoretical from realistic revenue.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: An investor deck that separates global TAM from the slice a startup can realistically serve in three years.

redFlags: Single authoritative report is the entire basis of the estimate
Percentages multiply to an answer without any unit or price anchor
No sanity check against analogous markets or historical revenue
classicProblems: Size the addressable market for an EV charging network in a given state
Estimate annual revenue opportunity for a new B2B analytics product
Compare TAM for two candidate go-to-market regions

paradigms.name: Competitive Analysis
paradigms.coreIdea: Characterize the structure of rivalry, substitution, and bargaining power to explain why profits accrue where they do in a market.
whenItApplies: Question asks whether a market is attractive to enter or remain in
Margin compression or share loss needs a structural explanation
Strategy must position against identifiable rivals or substitutes

subVariants.name: Porter-Style Industry Analysis
subVariants.keyMechanism: Evaluate five forces — rivalry, entrants, substitutes, buyer power, supplier power — to estimate structural profitability.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Explain why airlines persistently under-earn their cost of capital.

subVariants.name: Head-to-Head Competitor Teardown
subVariants.keyMechanism: Feature, pricing, and positioning comparison across a small set of direct rivals.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Compare three CRM vendors to identify defensible differentiation.

subVariants.name: Strategic Group Mapping
subVariants.keyMechanism: Cluster competitors on two strategic dimensions to find under-served positions.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Map airlines by route density and service class to locate a premium-regional gap.

redFlags: Competitor list is narrower than the set of real substitutes customers consider
Analysis treats current market shares as destiny
No attention to supplier or buyer concentration
classicProblems: Evaluate attractiveness of entering the meal-kit category
Explain margin erosion in low-cost airlines
Identify white space in the project-management software market

paradigms.name: Valuation
paradigms.coreIdea: Translate a stream of expected cash flows, adjusted for risk and time, into a present value or defensible price.
whenItApplies: A buy, sell, or hold recommendation is required
An investment thesis must produce a target price or fair-value range
Capital allocation decisions hinge on NPV or IRR

subVariants.name: Discounted Cash Flow (DCF)
subVariants.keyMechanism: Project free cash flows, apply a discount rate reflecting risk, add terminal value.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Value a mature public company from explicit 5-year projections and a perpetuity-growth terminal.

subVariants.name: Relative / Comparables Valuation
subVariants.keyMechanism: Apply multiples (EV/EBITDA, P/E, EV/Sales) from a peer set to the subject's metrics.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Price an IPO against listed peers trading at observable EV/Revenue multiples.

subVariants.name: Precedent Transactions
subVariants.keyMechanism: Use multiples paid in historical M&A deals for similar assets.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Benchmark a pending SaaS acquisition against the last 10 strategic deals in the category.

redFlags: Terminal value dominates the DCF — most of the answer is one assumption
Peer set is chosen to support a conclusion rather than for true comparability
Discount rate is asserted, not derived from capital structure and beta
classicProblems: Build a target price for a listed equity ahead of earnings
Value a private company for a minority investment
Decide whether a share buyback is accretive at today's price

paradigms.name: Forecasting
paradigms.coreIdea: Project a time-indexed metric forward using historical structure, drivers, and uncertainty bounds.
whenItApplies: A numeric prediction of revenue, demand, or cost is needed
Budget or capacity planning depends on a forward estimate
Comparing scenarios requires a base-case trajectory

subVariants.name: Driver-Based Forecast
subVariants.keyMechanism: Decompose the metric into causal drivers and project each separately.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Forecast revenue as pricing × volume × mix, each driven by a specific assumption.

subVariants.name: Time-Series Extrapolation
subVariants.keyMechanism: Fit trend, seasonality, and autocorrelation to historical values (ARIMA, Holt-Winters, Prophet).
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Forecast next quarter's daily active users from three years of history.

subVariants.name: Analog / Reference-Class Forecasting
subVariants.keyMechanism: Anchor on the observed trajectory of similar past cases, then adjust.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Forecast adoption of a new streaming service using the first-three-year curves of predecessors.

redFlags: Point forecast with no confidence interval or scenario band
Trend extrapolation across a structural break (pandemic, policy change)
Driver decomposition that double-counts the same effect in two lines
classicProblems: Project next-year revenue for the annual operating plan
Forecast quarterly active users for capacity planning
Estimate demand for a new product class in its first 24 months

paradigms.name: Causal Inference
paradigms.coreIdea: Distinguish the effect of an intervention from correlated confounders so that a claim of 'X caused Y' survives scrutiny.
whenItApplies: A campaign, policy, or feature change needs a measured effect
Observed correlations could be driven by selection, seasonality, or a common cause
A go/no-go decision depends on whether the lift is real

subVariants.name: Randomized Controlled Trial (A/B Test)
subVariants.keyMechanism: Random assignment removes confounding between treatment and outcome.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: A/B test a new checkout flow and measure the true conversion lift.

subVariants.name: Difference-in-Differences
subVariants.keyMechanism: Compare pre/post change in a treated group against a control group's parallel trend.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Measure the effect of a regional price change using untreated regions as control.

subVariants.name: Regression Discontinuity / Instrumental Variables
subVariants.keyMechanism: Exploit an external rule or shock that assigns treatment as-if randomly around a cutoff.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Estimate the impact of a loyalty-program threshold on repeat purchase rate.

redFlags: Conclusion rests on a post-hoc comparison with no control
Treatment and control groups differ systematically before the intervention
Outcome metric was chosen after seeing the data
classicProblems: Estimate incremental revenue from a paid-media campaign
Measure the impact of a pricing change on churn
Attribute a sales lift to a product launch versus seasonality

paradigms.name: Portfolio Optimization
paradigms.coreIdea: Allocate a constrained resource across competing opportunities to maximize a risk-adjusted objective.
whenItApplies: A fixed budget, headcount, or capital pool must be split among projects
Return and risk of each option can be estimated independently
The right mix — not the single best bet — is the decision

subVariants.name: Mean-Variance / Markowitz
subVariants.keyMechanism: Pick weights that maximize expected return for a given variance (or vice versa).
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Construct an equity portfolio on the efficient frontier.

subVariants.name: Capital Allocation / Project Portfolio
subVariants.keyMechanism: Rank projects by risk-adjusted NPV or IRR subject to budget and strategic constraints.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Allocate next year's R&D budget across 20 candidate initiatives.

subVariants.name: Marketing Mix Modeling
subVariants.keyMechanism: Allocate spend across channels to maximize incremental revenue given diminishing returns.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Split a $50M media budget across search, social, TV, and affiliate.

redFlags: Inputs (expected return, covariance) are measured with far more precision than warranted
Constraint set omits a real-world limit that the recommended allocation violates
Optimizer produces corner solutions that the business would never actually execute
classicProblems: Allocate marketing spend across channels for next fiscal year
Construct a risk-parity portfolio across asset classes
Prioritize a product roadmap under a fixed engineering budget

paradigms.name: Scenario Analysis
paradigms.coreIdea: Structure uncertainty as a small number of internally consistent futures so decisions can be stress-tested against each.
whenItApplies: Key drivers are deeply uncertain and interact
Stakeholders need more than a single point estimate
Decisions must be robust across a range of plausible worlds

subVariants.name: Base / Bull / Bear
subVariants.keyMechanism: Three scenarios defined by coordinated moves in the critical drivers.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Quarterly plan showing revenue under conservative, expected, and optimistic macro.

subVariants.name: 2x2 Scenario Matrix
subVariants.keyMechanism: Cross two orthogonal high-uncertainty axes into four narrative futures.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Futures for automotive industry across electrification rate and regulation intensity.

subVariants.name: Monte Carlo Simulation
subVariants.keyMechanism: Sample joint distributions over drivers to generate thousands of outcome paths.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Distribution of project NPV given uncertain volumes, prices, and costs.

redFlags: Scenarios are just 'plan ± 10%' with no narrative divergence
Drivers across scenarios are not internally consistent
Analysis ignores the scenario most damaging to current strategy
classicProblems: Stress-test a strategic plan across macro scenarios
Evaluate a capital project under commodity-price uncertainty
Prepare an investment committee memo with upside and downside cases

paradigms.name: Segmentation Analysis
paradigms.coreIdea: Partition a customer, product, or market population into groups whose behavior, needs, or economics differ meaningfully enough to warrant different actions.
whenItApplies: Averages across a population are misleading or unstable
Marketing, pricing, or product decisions should vary by group
Retention or profitability is highly heterogeneous across users

subVariants.name: Demographic / Firmographic Segmentation
subVariants.keyMechanism: Partition by observable traits (age, income, industry, size).
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Segment SMB customers by headcount band for sales-team coverage.

subVariants.name: Behavioral / RFM Segmentation
subVariants.keyMechanism: Group by observed actions — recency, frequency, monetary value, feature usage.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Identify 'at-risk high-value' e-commerce customers for a winback program.

subVariants.name: Needs / Jobs-to-be-Done Segmentation
subVariants.keyMechanism: Cluster by the outcome the customer is trying to achieve, not by who they are.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Segment productivity-software buyers by the underlying job they are hiring the tool for.

redFlags: Segments are not actionable — no distinct decision flows from them
Segments overlap heavily or are unstable across time
Segmentation is built on easy-to-measure traits rather than decision-relevant ones
classicProblems: Design a tiered pricing structure for a SaaS product
Identify the highest-value customer segment for a loyalty program
Choose which segments to target with a new product launch

paradigms.name: Funnel Analysis
paradigms.coreIdea: Model a sequential conversion process as stages with drop-off rates so bottlenecks and leverage points become visible.
whenItApplies: Outcome depends on a multi-step user or buyer journey
Need to diagnose where volume is lost between awareness and revenue
Campaign or product performance must be decomposed into stages

subVariants.name: Marketing Funnel (AARRR / TOFU-MOFU-BOFU)
subVariants.keyMechanism: Acquisition → activation → retention → revenue → referral, each with a rate.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Diagnose why paid traffic is not translating into paying customers.

subVariants.name: Sales Pipeline Funnel
subVariants.keyMechanism: MQL → SQL → Opportunity → Closed-Won with stage-specific conversion.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Identify the sales stage responsible for a missed quarterly number.

subVariants.name: Product Activation Funnel
subVariants.keyMechanism: Signup → first-value event → habit, measured step by step.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Locate the feature step where new users drop off before reaching 'aha'.

redFlags: Stages are defined by what is easy to instrument rather than by real decision moments
Drop-off is blamed on the last stage without checking upstream quality
Conversion rates are compared across cohorts of very different composition
classicProblems: Diagnose why a paid-ads campaign has poor end-to-end ROI
Find the biggest leverage point in a B2B sales pipeline
Evaluate onboarding changes for activation lift

paradigms.name: Macro / Trend Reading
paradigms.coreIdea: Interpret long-horizon shifts in technology, regulation, demographics, and behavior to anticipate durable changes in demand or competition.
whenItApplies: Decision horizon is longer than normal planning cycles
Structural, not cyclical, change is suspected
Strategy must withstand regime shifts, not just quarterly noise

subVariants.name: PESTEL Scan
subVariants.keyMechanism: Scan political, economic, social, technological, environmental, legal forces.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Assess long-term impact of carbon regulation on a heavy-industry portfolio.

subVariants.name: S-Curve / Diffusion Analysis
subVariants.keyMechanism: Locate a technology or behavior on its adoption curve to project its trajectory.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Estimate where EV adoption sits on its S-curve and when the inflection has passed.

subVariants.name: Leading-Indicator Synthesis
subVariants.keyMechanism: Combine early signals (patents, hiring, search interest, regulatory filings) into a directional read.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Call the direction of enterprise AI spending from hiring, funding, and procurement data.

redFlags: Trend narrative is built entirely from press coverage, not measurable indicators
Short-term volatility is mistaken for a structural trend
Analysis confuses what is interesting with what is material to the business
classicProblems: Assess whether AI will disrupt a professional-services category
Evaluate demographic risk to a consumer-goods portfolio
Call the durability of remote-work spend categories

paradigms.name: Comparable Benchmark
paradigms.coreIdea: Reason about an unknown quantity or outcome by anchoring on a reference class of analogous cases and adjusting for the ways the target differs.
whenItApplies: No historical data exists for the target but similar cases do
Estimate must hedge against single-source bias
Pre-revenue or greenfield decisions requiring a credible prior
Calibrating intuitions against peer outcomes before committing

subVariants.name: Reference-Class Forecasting
subVariants.keyMechanism: Identify a statistically relevant class of prior cases, take the distribution of outcomes, and locate the target within it before adjusting for idiosyncratic factors.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Forecast duration of a large IT project by comparing to a reference class of similar projects rather than building a bottom-up schedule.

subVariants.name: Peer-Adjustment Triangulation
subVariants.keyMechanism: Take multiple comparables, compute a central tendency and spread, then adjust toward the target using small, defensible multipliers.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Estimate conversion rate for a new open-source dev-tool landing page by averaging measured rates of comparable tools and adjusting for niche size.

subVariants.name: Before/After Analogue
subVariants.keyMechanism: Use a pre/post outcome from an analogous case to size the likely delta for the target.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Estimate revenue lift from adding a paid tier by looking at how peers' revenue shifted when they introduced similar tiers.

redFlags: Reference class is chosen to support a desired conclusion rather than for structural similarity
Single comparable is taken as the answer without a range or spread
No adjustment for the ways the target differs materially from the class
classicProblems: Estimate first-year GitHub Sponsor revenue for a niche dev tool before it launches
Size plausible conversion rate for a new OSS pricing page
Calibrate a pre-revenue startup's headcount plan against peer-stage companies

paradigms.name: Policy Design
paradigms.coreIdea: Design a set of rules, tiers, or mechanisms that shape behavior toward a chosen objective, and evaluate each option against the incentives and edge cases it creates.
whenItApplies: Decision produces a rulebook, pricing tier, license, or eligibility mechanism rather than a single number
Multiple stakeholders are affected and their responses differ
Enforcement, bypass, and compliance cost must be evaluated alongside the intended outcome
Trade-offs between simplicity, fairness, and incentive-compatibility must be made explicit

subVariants.name: Mechanism / Tier Design
subVariants.keyMechanism: Enumerate candidate tier splits, map who each tier rewards or discourages, and pick the split that best aligns user incentives with the objective.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Design a free-vs-paid feature split for an OSS desktop app that preserves community goodwill while making seats defensible.

subVariants.name: License / Rule Drafting
subVariants.keyMechanism: Select a legal or contractual instrument, enumerate what it permits and prohibits, and evaluate downstream effects on adoption and bypass risk.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Choose between AGPL, BSL, Elastic 2.0, and source-available custom for a database product.

subVariants.name: Enforcement / Compliance Design
subVariants.keyMechanism: Decide how a rule is observed or enforced (honor system, technical gating, audit) and trade enforcement cost against leakage.
subVariants.archetypalProblem: Decide whether a per-seat desktop license is enforced by license server, hardware ID, or honor-system activation code.

redFlags: Design optimizes the intended case without considering how sophisticated users will route around it
No stakeholder-by-stakeholder impact trace (buyer, free user, contributor, enterprise)
Enforcement cost is not weighed against the revenue or behavior it protects
classicProblems: Split free and paid features for a desktop companion to an OSS framework
Choose a dual-licensing model for an OSS database
Design a sponsorware gate that releases features after a funding milestone

crossParadigmGuide.signal: Question is 'how big is this opportunity' with no pricing decision
Question is 'should we enter this market' and profitability is the concern
Decision requires a price, target, or NPV
Need a forward numeric trajectory for planning
Observed lift must be defended as causal, not correlational
Fixed budget must be split across multiple bets
Deep uncertainty in macro or exogenous drivers
Population averages are hiding heterogeneous behavior
Conversion problem with an obvious multi-step journey
Decision horizon is 5+ years with structural change suspected
No historicals exist for the target but analogous cases do
Decision produces a rule, tier, or mechanism rather than a single number
